connection: OrderService no longer has the responsibility of creating notifications. Factory owns creation. Service owns business logic. Each class has one reason to change.
example: Before: OrderService creates + sends notifications. After: Factory creates, OrderService just uses them.

connection: Add WhatsApp? Create a new class + add one line in factory. OrderService, PaymentService, ShippingService stay CLOSED for modification.
example: 10 services use NotificationFactory.create(). Adding WhatsApp changes only the factory. 10 services untouched.

connection: Client depends on Notification (abstraction), not EmailNotification (concretion). The factory provides the right object without the client knowing the concrete class.
example: OrderService holds Notification (interface). Factory decides the concrete type. OrderService is decoupled from all implementations.

connection: All products returned by the factory are substitutable through the interface. Email, SMS, Push all satisfy the Notification contract.
example: NotificationFactory.create(EMAIL) and NotificationFactory.create(SMS) both return Notification -- client uses them identically.
